pr.title: Remove unused stack `#if` guards and todo's
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Large mechanical cleanup that removed obsolete `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` wrappers and `/// @todo Unused stack.` comments around unused stack-padding local arrays. The PR touched 219 files, added no lines, and deleted 2626 lines while preserving the stack arrays themselves, e.g. `u8 _[8];`, `u8 _[16];`, and initialized variants such as `u8 _[16] = { 0 };`. The change reduced preprocessor noise across Dolphin/MSL, fighter, item, stage, game-mode, player, and baselib code without evidence of functional rewrites.
what_changed: Removed obsolete `/// @todo Unused stack.` annotations from many functions where local dummy stack arrays are intentionally present.
Removed `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` / `#endif` guards that previously enclosed unused stack-padding declarations.
Kept the unused stack arrays themselves in place, preserving declarations such as `u8 _[8];`, `u8 _[16];`, `u8 _[40];`, and `u8 _[16] = { 0 };`.
Applied the cleanup broadly across 219 changed files with 0 added lines and 2626 deleted lines.

Representative Dolphin/MSL functions cleaned include `__write_console`, `__read_console`, `__AI_SRC_INIT`, `__DBExceptionDestinationAux`, `__DSPHandler`, `__OSInitAudioSystem`, `__OSInitMemoryProtection`, `OSResetSystem`, `__OSGetSystemTime`, and `__OSTimeToSystemTime`.

Representative fighter code cleaned includes Captain special move callbacks, common action callbacks, CLink init/item callbacks, Ness/Mewtwo/Pikachu/Luigi/Samus/Fox/etc. special move files, and many ftCommon action files.
Some functions inside non-ASM C bodies or `#ifndef MUST_MATCH` sections also had nested unused-stack `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` blocks removed, leaving the stack locals directly in the function body.
smart_moves: The PR was intentionally deletion-only: it removed comments and conditional wrappers while leaving the matching-relevant stack padding declarations untouched.
By making the dummy stack locals unconditional, the source becomes easier to read and avoids scattering match-only preprocessor branches around otherwise normal C code.
The cleanup was applied mechanically across many small hunks, which is appropriate for a repeated pattern like `/// @todo Unused stack.` followed by `#ifdef MUST_MATCH`.
Preserving exact array sizes and initializers was important: the diff shows the padding variables remain as evidence that the change was about source hygiene, not stack layout experimentation.
decomp_lessons: When a dummy local array is still needed for stack layout or matching, remove stale TODO wording only if the array itself remains and its size/initializer is preserved.
Do not treat every unused local as dead code in decomp work; in this PR, `u8 _[...]` locals are intentional stack-shaping artifacts.
Once a matching workaround is accepted as normal source structure, wrapping it in `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` can become counterproductive noise.
For broad cleanup PRs, keep the edit pattern narrow and uniform so reviewers can verify that no logic changed.
Deletion-only preprocessor cleanup is safest when the before/after diff shows the same C statements still present outside the removed guards.
naming_conventions: Dummy stack-padding arrays commonly use the short placeholder name `_`, e.g. `u8 _[8];`.
Some preexisting padding locals used a descriptive placeholder such as `unused`, e.g. `u8 unused[8];`; this PR did not rename them.
The repeated comment text removed was `/// @todo Unused stack.`.
The guard macro removed around these locals was `MUST_MATCH`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit local byte arrays to force or preserve stack frame size/alignment when needed for matching.
Keep exact dummy array sizes intact during cleanup: examples in the diff include 4, 8, 12, 16, 24, 28, 32, and 40 byte arrays.
Initialized dummy arrays such as `u8 _[16] = { 0 };` should retain their initializer if it was part of the matching behavior.
Removing `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` around stack-padding locals can normalize matching scaffolding into the regular C source without changing function logic.
The diff provides no evidence of new asm-to-C conversions; this was cleanup around existing matched or matching-oriented functions.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale was available; conclusions are based on the PR title, file counts, and diff excerpt.
searchable_terms: Remove unused stack if guards and todos
follow_up_queries: Find remaining `/// @todo Unused stack.` comments after PR 1111.
Find remaining `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` blocks that only guard dummy stack arrays.
Audit `u8 _[...]` stack-padding locals for exact size preservation before cleanup.
Search for initialized dummy stack arrays `= { 0 }` that may be matching-sensitive.
Check whether later PRs removed or renamed dummy stack locals after this cleanup.
